title: Boat Rental in Dubai: 2026 Costs, Seasons & Tips
html:
Renting a boat in Dubai lets you explore the Arabian Gulf on your own terms. Depart from Dubai Marina for a half-day trip, with prices starting at around 1,200 AED for a small motorboat. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Dubai or list your own.

Where to set off in Dubai

Dubai offers several world-class departure points. The most popular is Dubai Marina, a purpose-built canal city with direct access to the Gulf. Alternatively, you can launch from Palm Jumeirah’s East Marina, close to the Atlantis resort. For a more traditional setting, the historic Dubai Creek is ideal for dhow cruises. Port Rashid, now home to several yacht clubs, is another excellent option. Finally, the new Dubai Harbour, near Palm Jumeirah, provides modern facilities and easy access to the open sea. Each marina offers its own character and proximity to key landmarks. One option worth a look is the Limpopo listing.

Which boat to rent

Your choice of boat depends on the size of your group and the type of experience you want. Many options are available through boat hire Dubai listings. Here are four popular types:

  1. Speedboat Best for a thrilling short trip of two hours, carrying up to 10 guests, from 1,200 AED.
  2. Motor yacht Ideal for day cruises with groups of up to 12 guests, from 3,500 AED for a half-day.
  3. Catamaran Perfect for stable, spacious sailing with up to 20 guests, from 5,000 AED for a full day.
  4. Luxury superyacht For executive entertaining, carrying up to 40 guests, from 15,000 AED per half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Dubai?

Boat rental prices in Dubai range from 400 AED for a shared tour to over 20,000 AED for a crewed superyacht. These costs vary by boat size, duration, and season. Typical price bands include:

  • Shared speedboat tour: from 400 AED per person for two hours.
  • Small motorboat (half-day): 1,200 to 2,500 AED for up to 8 guests.
  • Catamaran (full-day): 5,000 to 8,000 AED for up to 20 guests.
  • Crewed motor yacht (full-day): 10,000 to 18,000 AED including crew and fuel.

All prices exclude gratuities and optional extras. Booking directly from verified owners can help you compare verified boat listings and find the best deal.

When to rent a boat in Dubai

The best season for boating in Dubai is from October to April, when daytime temperatures are mild. During these months, the sea is calm and the Shamal wind occasionally picks up, making for good sailing conditions if you are on a catamaran. Summer months (June to September) are extremely hot, but air-conditioned yachts remain available. Rental prices in summer can drop by 30 to 40 per cent. Always check weather forecasts before departing, as wind and heat can change quickly.

‘On a Friday afternoon in January, a gentle breeze off Palm Jumeirah makes for a perfect sunset cruise. Book a yacht with a shaded deck to enjoy the winter sun.’

At a glance

  • Price range: 400, 20,000+ AED per trip
  • Peak months: October to April
  • Typical trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Max capacity: 8 to 150 guests
  • Currency: AED (United Arab Emirates dirham)

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Dubai?

In Dubai, you do not need a licence to charter a crewed boat, but you do need one to drive unskippered. Most rental boats on BoatHire24 come with a captain, so no licence is required. If you want to self-drive, you must hold a valid international boat licence and present it to the operator. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. For jet skis, a Dubai driving licence or a one-day permit from the marina is needed.

Questions about boat rental in Dubai

Can I rent a boat in Dubai without a licence?

Yes, you can rent a crewed boat without any licence; the captain handles everything.

How far is the Burj Al Arab from Dubai Marina by boat?

The Burj Al Arab is approximately 5 nautical miles from Dubai Marina, a 20-minute cruise along the coast.

Are there luxury yachts available for charter in Dubai?

Yes, you can charter luxury yachts such as the 26m Maiora and 55m Azimut from Dubai Marina and Port Rashid.

What is included in the price of a boat rental in Dubai?

Typically, the price includes the boat, fuel, captain, and soft drinks; food and gratuities are extra.

BoatHire24 connects you with verified boat owners in Dubai and around the world. Whether you want a speedboat for two hours or a superyacht for a week, explore options on boathire24.com. If you own a vessel, you can list it and earn extra income in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dubai and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
